calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2026 Continued To Aug-19-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sarkis Sirmabekian, Esq. In Support;
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Prior to the hearing, Atty. Sirmabekian appears via Zoom. The court received a late notice to contest. At the Court's instruction, the matter is not heard. The Court's tentative ruling becomes the final ruling of the Court.

No appearancesin Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff George Nikitas's Notice of Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Plaintiff is ordered to submit by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order consistent with this tentative forthwith.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sarkis Sirmabekian, Esq. In Support;
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Grigor Sirmabekhian, Esq. 818-473-5003 Sirmabekhian Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Lee Sheldon, Esq. 415-216-1239 Littler for Defendant Garuda Labs and Advanate Workforce Solutions.

RULING - The matter is Not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

2 - PLAINTIFF GEORGE NIKITAS' Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss.

Continued on the court's own motion to be heard on June 1, 2026.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Grigor Sirmabekhian, Esq. 818-473-5003 Sirmabekhian Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Lee Sheldon, Esq. 415-216-1239 Littler for Defendant Garuda Labs and Advanate Workforce Solutions.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Sheldon contests and presents arguments; Counsel contests the Court's finding that the public's interest in ensuring a PAGA settlement is fair and reasonable outweighs the Parties' interest in confidentiality, requests dismissal of civil action sent to arbitration.
Court comments and poses questions; fairness to the public, PAGA claim have different rules, balancing public's right to access.
Mr. Sirmabekhian has nothing further to add.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

1 - Defendants Garuda Labs, Inc. and Advantage Workforce Services, LLC's unopposed Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal is DENIED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. The Court raises a threshold issue; cana prty be self-represented in a PAGA claim. Mr. Sheldon responds; informs the court of the situation re arbitration; dismiss without prejudice, resolution as to individual claims, no PAGA settlement to be approved, only individual claims ordered to arbitration, substitution of attorney (self-executing) filed after the agreement. The court states there will be an order striking that. The court will prepare an order striking the substitution and attorney will be back as counsel of record to explain.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff George Nikitas's Notice of Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The Court will prepare an order striking the substitution of attorney.

Plaintiff George Nikitas's Notice of Motion For An Order For Entry Of Joint Stipulation To Dismiss is DENIED without Prejudice.

Plaintiff fails to identify a statutory or common law basis for his motion, which, alone is grounds for denial. Plaintiff must identify the legal basis for his request, the applicable legal standard for adjudication and an analysis thereon.

Code of Civil Procedure section 1005 governs certain procedural requirements for noticed motions. It is not a substantive basis for a request for a court order. Local Rule 8.2, similarly, is off point.

If the motion is made under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1), the court will not authorize dismissal absent more information, in light of the fact that dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. It is not enough that the parties may desire court approval.

If the motion is Labor Code section 2699(s), the court needs much more information and analysis. "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part." (Labor Code section 2699(s)(2).) Here, Plaintiff, who accepted the role of public prosecutor for purposes of the representative claim(s), must (at the very least) submit the settlement agreement for the individual claim(s), explain why the settlement should not be construed as consideration for dismissing the representative claims, and explain how dismissal of the representative claims is in the public interest. Review of the settlement agreement is largely for the benefit of the aggrieved employees, who are members of the public entitled to access to court records.

Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This request for dismissal of the representative claim(s) is based on settlement of Plaintiff's individual claim. Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that he submitted his papers to the LWDA. It is unlikely the court will approve dismissal of the representative claims on these grounds on a subsequent motion absent notice, and proof thereof, to the LWDA. Notice should be provided consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b). It is unlikely, as well, that the court will seal the adjudicative facts, such as the provisions of the settlement agreement, necessary to its determination, particularly given the public's and aggrieved parties' interests.

As a side note, Plaintiff states aggrieved employees will suffer no prejudice from dismissal

The court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
